title: تعديل مخطط معماري لأرض غير منتظمة
skills: AUTOCAD | AutoCAD | 2D Drafting | Building Design | Architecture | Floor Plan
full_description:
لدي أرض غير منتظمة الأضلاع (مرفق صورة الكروكي بأبعاد وأطوال الأرض)، ولدي مخطط مبدئي للدور الأرضي (مرفق صورة المخطط). المخطط الحالي جيد ولكنه يحتاج إلى إعادة دراسة وتعديل جذري من قبل مهندس معماري محترف لحل بعض الإشكاليات وتلبية احتياجاتي الجديدة.
الاحتياجات والمكونات المطلوبة في المخطط (نفس المخطط المرفق + إضافة):
1 مجلس رجال + دورة مياه ومغاسل تابعة له.
2 غرفة طعام.
3 صالة عائلية واسعة.
4 مطبخ.
5 غرفة نوم إضافية (هذا هو المطلب الأساسي الجديد).
6 دورة مياه تخدم الصالة وغرفة النوم.
7 منطقة الدرج.
ملاحظات واشتراطات هامة جداً للمصمم (يرجى قراءتها بعناية قبل تقديم العرض):
 لا أريد أي "شطرات" (زوايا مائلة) داخل المبنى نهائياً
 الا اذا كان ذالك ضروري ولا مفر منه كيفية : يمكن للمصمم استيعاب الشطرات في الارتدادات الخارجية (الحوش)، أو استغلالها في تصميم مساحات غير رئيسية مثل: (مستودع صغير، غرفة غسيل، مناور، أو دواليب حائطية مخفية) بحيث يبقى الشكل الداخلي للغرف منتظماً.
 مرونة في التصميم: أنا مستعد لتقبل التضحية بمساحات بعض الغرف وتصغيرها بشكل معقول، ولدي مرونة تامة في إعادة ترتيب أماكن الغرف بالكامل (تغيير أماكن المجلس، الصالة، المطبخ.. الخ) إذا كان ذلك سيخدم فكرة إضافة الغرفة الجديدة ويضمن لي التخلص من الشطرات بشكل متناغم وذكي.
 يجب اخذ التهوية بعين الاعتبار المطلوب تسليمه:
 مسقط أفقي (Floor Plan) ثنائي الأبعاد (2D) يوضح التوزيع المعماري الجديد.
 تسليم الملفات بصيغة PDF وأيضاً الملف المصدري AutoCAD (DWG).
المرفقات:
1 صورة كروكي الأرض (بالمقاسات والزوايا).
2 صورة المخطط المبدئي الحالي.

title: Specialty Coffee Branding
skills: BRANDING | Branding | Packaging Design | Visual Design | Typography | Logo Design | Graphic Design | Illustration | Illustrator
full_description:
I am launching a new specialty coffee roastery and I am looking for a strong designer to help develop the visual identity.

The brand is very personal and built around independence, maturity, precision, and calm confidence. It should feel established, peaceful, simple, premium, and trustworthy — not loud, childish, cartoonish, or overly trendy.

I need help with:

* Logo
* Basic visual identity direction
* Colour palette
* Coffee bag / packaging concept
* Label layout for rotating coffees

The design should feel modern, refined, and distinctive, with strong shelf presence. I want the packaging to stand out without looking like typical loud specialty coffee branding.

The logo should be simple, memorable, and work well across coffee bags, labels, boxes, website, social media, documents, signage, and merchandise. I do not want generic coffee icons such as beans, cups, flames, mountains, or cartoon illustrations.

The tone of the brand should be:

Calm, confident, precise, honest, personal, mature, and independent.

Visual direction I like:

* Minimal but not boring
* Premium but not cold
* Strong typography
* Confident use of one main colour
* Matte / tactile / refined packaging feel
* Clean information hierarchy
* Scandinavian / European restraint
* Strong shelf impact

Visual direction to avoid:

* Cartoonish design
* Childish graphics
* Loud specialty coffee clichés
* Generic coffee symbols
* Trend-led design
* Overly playful branding
* Anything that feels cheap, random, or trying too hard

The final design system should be practical for a small roastery launching with rotating coffees, so it needs to be beautiful but also easy to use and repeat.

Please include examples of previous branding or packaging work, especially in coffee, food, drink, hospitality, or premium consumer goods.

Deliverables required:

* Logo files
* Brand colour palette
* Typography recommendations
* Packaging concept
* Label layout
* Basic brand guidelines
* Editable source files

I am looking for someone with strong taste, restraint, and the ability to create a serious, recognisable brand — not just a quick logo.

title: Android App Functional Testing -- 2
skills: API TESTING | API Testing | Test Automation | Android App Development | Android Studio | Android | Mobile App Development | Testing / QA | Mobile App Testing | Reverse Engineering
full_description:
I have an Android app that’s feature-complete and now needs full functional validation at the system level. The main objective is to exercise every user-visible workflow end-to-end and confirm that all API interactions behave exactly as designed—success paths, edge cases, and graceful failure handling when the network misbehaves.

What I will provide 
• The latest APK (or Play Store internal-test link) 
• Swagger / Postman collection for the REST endpoints 
• High-level user stories and acceptance criteria

What I expect back 
• A concise test plan outlining the scenarios you will cover 
• Executed test cases with pass/fail results, screenshots or screen-captures for any defect found, and clear reproduction steps 
• A structured bug list (severity, steps, expected vs. actual, device/OS) ready for Jira or similar 
• Final summary report highlighting coverage, open issues, and regression recommendations

Key focus areas 
• System-level functional testing on real devices and at least one emulator 
• Thorough validation of all API calls: authentication headers, payload accuracy, response codes, timeouts, and offline/airplane-mode behaviour 
• Confirmation that UI feedback matches backend status (loading states, error toasts, retry logic)

Preferred toolset (use what you’re comfortable with): Android Studio logcat, Postman, Charles/Fiddler for traffic inspection, and any mobile test frameworks such as Espresso or Robotium if they speed things up.

I’m looking to kick this off immediately and iterate quickly until we reach a clean test pass. Let me know your device pool and estimated turnaround once you’ve reviewed the details.

title: Compact 12V Boost Converter PCB
skills: BILL OF MATERIALS (BOM) ANALYSIS | Bill of Materials (BOM) Analysis | Motor Control | PCB Layout | Electronics | Electrical Engineering | Circuit Design
full_description:
Project Overview 
We are looking for an experienced PCB designer to develop a compact production ready DC-DC boost converter PCB for our RC product lineup. The PCB size is fixed and must match our existing RC chassis mounting system. 
The board will take input from a single 3.7V 18650 Li-ion battery and boost it to a stable constant 12V output capable of driving either BO motors or N20 motors continuously without major voltage drop under load. 
This is intended for mass production, so the design must be reliable, cost optimized, manufacturable, and use easily available components. 
The final design must be fully production ready and suitable for commercial manufacturing. 

PCB Size and Mechanical Constraints 
PCB dimensions are fixed: 
60mm x 38mm 
All components must be placed only on the top side of the PCB. 
The placement of JST connectors and capacitor positions are fixed to match our existing RC chassis and 
locking system. 
Please strictly follow the provided coordinates and orientations. 
PCB Functional Requirements 
Main Function 
Input: 
Single 3.7V 18650 Li-ion battery 
Output: 
Stable constant 12V DC output 
The board should: 
• Boost 3.7V battery voltage to regulated 12V 
• Maintain stable output voltage under motor load 
• Power 2 DC motors continuously 
• Power 2 external 3V LEDs 
• Turn ON and OFF using an external switch connected through JST connector 
• LEDs should turn ON immediately when PCB is powered 
• Motors should start running immediately when PCB is powered 
 
Connectors and Fixed Positions 
JST Connector Details 
Connector Part Number: 
B2B-XH-A(LF)(SN) 
All connectors are through hole type.
Capacitor Placement Constraints 
We currently have locking structures in our RC chassis based on capacitor positions from our old PCB. 
You may use 2 or 3 capacitors as needed for the design, but please place them within the following 
locations as much as possible. 
Maintaining similar capacitor size is preferred. 
Capacitor Footprint: 
Capacitor_SMD:CP_Elec_8x11.9 
Capacitor Part Number: 
JVE16V470M8X10 
Motor Specifications 
The designer must properly calculate current requirements and select suitable ICs, inductors, capacitors, 
and track widths. 
BO Motor Specifications 
Operating Voltage: 
3V to 12V 
No Load Current: 
40mA to 180mA 
Rated Speed: 
60 RPM 
Rated Torque: 
1 kg·cm 
N20 Motor Specifications 
Operating Voltage: 
6V to 12V 
Rated Voltage: 
12V 
Rated Speed: 
60 RPM 
Stall Current: 
0.75A 
Rated Torque: 
0.97 kg·cm 
Stall Torque: 
3.0 kg·cm 
Design Requirements 
Important Requirements 
• Output voltage must remain stable at 12V under motor load 
• Avoid major voltage drop during startup or load changes 
• Use proper inductors and efficient boost converter IC 
• Track widths must support required motor current safely 
• Design must be suitable for long term operation 
• Use low cost and easily available components 
• Manufacturing cost target for PCB assembly should remain below Rs.100 if possible 
• Avoid expensive or difficult to source components 
• Design should be optimized for mass production 
• Use proper grounding and power routing practices 
• Thermal performance should be considered 
• All SMD components should be placed on top side only 
Deliverables Required 
The freelancer must provide all production files required for manufacturing. 
Required files: 
• Complete schematic design 
• PCB layout design 
• Editable KiCad project files 
• Gerber files 
• Drill files 
• BOM 
• Pick and Place files 
• 3D PCB files 
• PDF schematics 
• PCB fabrication outputs 
• Assembly files 
• Any additional files required for manufacturing 
Workflow Requirement 
Step 1: 
Designer must first create and share schematic design for approval. 
Step 2: 
Only after schematic approval should PCB layout design begin. 
Preferred Skills 
Looking for a designer experienced in: 
• DC-DC boost converter design 
• Power electronics 
• Battery powered PCB systems 
• KiCad PCB design 
• High current PCB routing 
• Manufacturing ready PCB development 
• Cost optimized electronics design 
Additional Notes 
This PCB must fit inside our existing RC product enclosure, so connector and capacitor placement 
accuracy is very important. 
The design should be clean, practical, and production ready, not just a prototype level design. 
Please share previous similar power supply or boost converter PCB work while applying.
description_snippet: Project Overview We are looking for an experienced PCB designer to develop a compact production ready DC-DC boost converter PCB for our RC product lineup. The PCB size is fixed and must match our existing RC chassis mounting system. The board will ta

title: Forecasting Pipeline & React Integration
skills: MACHINE LEARNING (ML) | Machine Learning (ML) | Python | Terraform | Data Processing | JSON | AngularJS | Redis | API Development
full_description:
I have the [login to view URL] React + TanStack Start UI already in place; now I need it wired to a real-time forecasting backend that can service 100 stores and roughly 10 k SKUs per store from a daily POS feed.

Scope 
• Ingest the feed in CSV first, with the code structured so we can swap to JSON or Parquet later without touching business logic. 
• Store raw and feature-engineered data in partitioned Parquet inside S3, queried locally through DuckDB/Polars and cached in Redis. 
• Train and serve forecasts with LightGBM and Croston/TSB, orchestrated by Airflow. 
• Ship a single-node pipeline that is production-ready yet cleanly abstracted for a 2–4 week lift to PySpark on EMR Serverless when volumes grow. 
• Expose the pipeline through createServerFn so the existing React storefront can request forecasts in real time.

What must be fully exercised by end-to-end tests (Playwright + Vitest + pytest): 
• Data ingestion & processing 
• Machine-learning prediction paths 
• Data storage & retrieval 
• UI-to-API round-trips across the entire flow

Deliverables 
1. Terraform definitions and IAM policies for S3, Redis/ElastiCache, Lambda endpoints and Airflow. 
2. Python package (DuckDB/Polars, LightGBM, Croston) with unit tests and typed docs. 
3. Airflow DAGs ready to deploy, parameterised for store/SKU segmentation. 
4. TypeScript server adapters that plug straight into the TanStack Start frontend. 
5. Playwright, Vitest and pytest suites running in CI, green from ingestion to on-screen forecast. 
6. A concise migration guide outlining what changes when we switch the compute engine to Spark.

Please bid only if you have hands-on experience scaling a single-node ML pipeline to Spark and can share references of similar projects.

title: Single Date Update in PDF
skills: ADOBE ACROBAT | Adobe Acrobat | Adobe Illustrator | Print Design | Typography | PDF | Adobe InDesign | Illustrator | Graphic Design
full_description:
I have a finished PDF that is ready to go except for one detail: a single date shown in the body text still reads in the DD/MM/YYYY format and must be replaced with the new date I will supply once we start. Nothing else in the file should move, resize, or lose quality—the layout, fonts, and resolution must remain exactly as‐is.

You’re free to use Adobe Acrobat, Illustrator, InDesign, or any other reliable PDF-editing tool, so long as the final file returns to me looking pixel-perfect and fully editable if it was editable before. Please send back:

• The corrected PDF 
• An outlined-fonts version (optional but appreciated) 
• A brief note confirming what was changed

I expect this to be a quick task for someone comfortable with precise PDF edits and version control. Let me know your turnaround time and I’ll forward the file right away.

title: Informational Business Website & SEO
skills: WEB DEVELOPMENT | Web Development | WordPress | Web Design | CMS | Website Design | SEO | Graphic Design | HTML
full_description:
I’m ready to launch a small business website whose sole purpose is to give visitors clear, trustworthy information about our company. I already know an “About Us” page is essential, and I’m happy to hear your recommendations on whether a Home, Contact, or any other page would improve navigation and credibility.

You’ll take the project from zero to live, handling design, development, hosting configuration, and fundamental search-engine optimisation so the site is easy to find the moment it goes online. A lightweight, easily editable CMS such as WordPress, Webflow, or a similar platform is preferred for ongoing updates.

Deliverables 
• Fully responsive website built on the agreed CMS 
• At least one complete “About Us” page, with room for expansion 
• On-page SEO: keyword research, meta titles/descriptions, alt tags, schema where appropriate 
• XML sitemap and [login to view URL], submitted to Google Search Console 
• Basic performance tuning to meet core-web-vital standards 
• Handover of all credentials, theme files, and a brief how-to guide for future edits

Acceptance criteria 
The site must load cleanly on mobile and desktop, score green on Google’s PageSpeed Insights, and be indexed in Google with no critical errors.

If this sounds like your wheelhouse, tell me how you’ll approach the build and SEO rollout, along with a rough timeline.

IDEAL CANDIDATE
This role is ideal for:
A graduate student
Advanced marketing student
Emerging digital marketer
Social media/content specialist building experience
Someone organized, creative, and reliable
I do not need a large agency or expensive senior strategist.

WORK STYLE

Organized

Calm under direction

Interested in transformational, educational, or personal development content
Strong communication skills are important.
TIME COMMITMENT

5-10 hours/week initially
Potential to expand later
Flexible remote work.
BUDGET
This is a startup-stage project.
I am specifically seeking:
affordable emerging talent

title: RingCentral Setup for New Startup U-S-based (ONLY)
skills: PROJECT MANAGEMENT | Project Management | Customer Support | Cloud Computing | Technical Support | VoIP | Telecommunications Engineering | Account Management | Twilio | Business Consulting | System Administration
full_description:
I am launching a new business in the United States and need an experienced, U-S-based professional to get our RingCentral account fully up and running. The goal is to hand over a clean, correctly configured system that my team can start using immediately for voice, SMS, and basic call routing.

Here is what I expect from the engagement:
• Complete initial account creation with the correct company details and U.S. address verification 
• Configuration of main company number, extensions, voicemail, and call flow so incoming calls reach the right person every time 
• Basic auto-attendant greeting and after-hours rules 
• Confirmation that emergency (E911) information is filed properly for all extensions 
• A short hand-off session (video or in person if you are nearby) showing me where critical settings live

Because this is for a brand-new enterprise, there are no legacy systems to migrate. At this stage I do not need integrations or user training beyond the brief walkthrough described above. 

When you apply, focus on your direct experience setting up RingCentral (or similar VoIP platforms) for U.S. businesses—particularly any cases where you handled the full initial build. If you have certifications or can share a quick success story, that will help me choose quickly.

I am ready to begin as soon as I find the right expert who can demonstrate clear, hands-on RingCentral setup expertise within the United States.
